subject: Geology
title: Cenozoic Volcanism
description: A quick, practical guide to Cenozoic Volcanism.

hook.text: The Cenozoic built the Ring of Fire, the Cascades, and Yellowstone. Volcanic activity reshaped continents and climate.

buildup.text: Subduction zones around the Pacific created volcanic arcs. Hotspots like Yellowstone built massive caldera systems.

discovery.text: The Deccan Traps erupted around the K-Pg boundary. Some scientists argue they contributed to the dinosaur extinction.

twist.text: Yellowstone's supervolcano last erupted 640,000 years ago. It could erupt again, blanketing North America in ash.

climax.text: Cenozoic volcanism shaped coastlines, climate, and even mass extinctions. Fire from below continues to reshape Earth.

punchline.text: The fire beneath us never stops building.

quiz.question: What is the Yellowstone supervolcano?
options: A massive caldera system that could erupt again
A dormant volcano on the ocean floor
